mcqs.question: The F-distribution is defined as the ratio of two independent ______ variables each divided by their degrees of freedom.
mcqs.explanation: By definition, if X1 ~ chi-square(d1) and X2 ~ chi-square(d2) are independent, then F = (X1/d1)/(X2/d2) follows the F-distribution with d1 and d2 degrees of freedom.

mcqs.question: What is the mean of an F-distribution with numerator degrees of freedom d1 = 5 and denominator degrees of freedom d2 = 12?
mcqs.explanation: The mean of the F-distribution is E(F) = d2/(d2 - 2) = 12/(12 - 2) = 12/10 = 1.2, valid for d2 > 2. The mean does not depend on d1.

mcqs.question: If T follows a t-distribution with 15 degrees of freedom, then T^2 follows which distribution?

options.text: F-distribution with 15 and 15 degrees of freedom
F-distribution with 1 and 15 degrees of freedom
Normal distribution with mean 0 and variance 1

mcqs.explanation: A well-known result states that if T ~ t(n), then T^2 ~ F(1, n). So T^2 follows an F-distribution with 1 numerator and 15 denominator degrees of freedom.

mcqs.question: Two independent samples of sizes 10 and 8 are drawn from normal populations. The sample variances are 25 and 10 respectively. What is the computed F-statistic for testing the equality of population variances?
mcqs.explanation: F = (larger variance)/(smaller variance) = 25/10 = 2.5. The larger variance is placed in the numerator by convention, with d1 = 10 - 1 = 9 and d2 = 8 - 1 = 7.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of the F-distribution?

options.text: It is symmetric about its mean
Its shape depends on two degrees of freedom parameters
The reciprocal of an F(d1, d2) variable follows F(d2, d1)

mcqs.explanation: The F-distribution is positively skewed (right-skewed), NOT symmetric. Options A, C, and D are all correct properties of the F-distribution.
